title: pseudoberlinia globiflora
intro: Pseudoberlinia globiflora, also known as the "African tree of life," is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is characterized by its large, globular flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In traditional medicine, Pseudoberlinia globiflora is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its extracts have also been used in the formulation of various herbal supplements and remedies.
